mnemonics: SR Latch: Set and Reset are Like a light switch (on/off) but can't be both at once.
JK's Job is to Kill the 'not allowed' state; it just Toggles when both are 1.
D-Flip-Flop: Data in, Delay out (on clock edge); it's a one-bit Delay line.
keyPoints: Flip-flops are the fundamental building blocks of sequential logic; they have memory.
Their output depends not only on current inputs but also on the previous state (history).
They change state based on a clock signal (edge-triggered) or enable signal (level-sensitive).
commonMistakes: Applying a forbidden input combination (e.g., S=R=1 in a basic SR latch).
Confusing level-triggered (transparent latch) and edge-triggered (flip-flop) behavior.
Forgetting the propagation delay; the output does not change instantaneously after the clock edge.
